title: Browser Origins, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Fields
description: A browser API reflects arbitrary Origin with credentials, mutates state on GET, accepts ambient cookies cross-site, caches preflight incorrectly, and treats CORS as server authorization. Learners cumulatively produce browser request trust and cross-origin policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, CORS read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization.
Validate exact origins, methods, requested headers, credential mode, exposed headers, max age, Vary, null origin, and rejection behavior.
Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.
